chunks.title: Introduction to Weight Loss
chunks.summary: The podcast opens with a discussion on how to lose weight effectively by challenging the conventional Western dietary patterns that contribute to obesity. It emphasizes the importance of dietary quality over calorie restriction for sustainable weight loss.

a helpful podcast for you on

chunks.title: How Not to Lose Weight
chunks.summary: The speaker elaborates on the pitfalls of common weight loss strategies that prioritize mere calorie reduction, emphasizing that such methods often lead to poor health outcomes and unsustainable weight loss. It highlights the importance of hormonal health during weight loss efforts.
chunks.transcript: your weight loss journey onto the podcast i believe that all discussions of how to lose weight probably start with how not to lose weight let's just start the whole conversation around how to lose weight with the understanding that the point of losing weight is to become healthier for some people the point of losing weight is to look a certain way and they may not prioritize overall health and hormonal robustness and health i'm thinking of bodybuilders here and that's their legitimate endeavor if they want to do certain things calorie restrict macronutrient adjust to get down to very low levels of body fat and they obviously have lots of muscle because they're bodybuilding to look a certain way that is fine but that is not a healthy phenotype that is not a healthy state for a human it is obvious and clear to anyone who's looked at the hormones and the overall health of people who are doing those bodybuilding shows that is not a healthy condition for a human so let's assume for the purposes of this podcast that our discussion is about how to lose weight healthfully and how to lose weight with the intention of being healthier i think that all conversations of how to lose weight should start with how to become a healthier individual and following beyond that will be weight loss i believe that if you become healthier you will lose weight so that you should focus on being healthier and i will outline what i think that means from a dietary and lifestyle and exercise perspective in this podcast but i do not think the goal of any endeavor for humans should be to lose weight nevertheless if that is your goal focus on becoming healthy again i will outline that in this podcast how to not lose weight is to become less healthy while you are losing weight i think if more people

chunks.title: Understanding Western Diets
chunks.summary: The podcast explains the detrimental effects of a Westernized dietary pattern, including the consumption of processed foods, refined sugars, and seed oils, on metabolism and obesity. The speaker contrasts this with more natural eating habits seen in hunter-gatherer societies.
chunks.transcript: checked morning testosterone cortisol looked at those ratios looked at tsh thyroid stimulating hormone t4 and t3 perhaps reverse t3 levels during their weight loss efforts it would give them a feedback it would give them some sort of a triangulation that would tell them if they are doing it in a healthful way all of these things are going to change as the body loses weight but i think if there are massive changes in hormonal function especially thyroid function to slow the thyroid and have a negative effect on the thyroid then what you are doing to lose weight is probably not sustainable long term and is probably not good for your overall health similarly if you are losing weight and your testosterone crashes because you are fasting or you are consistently calorie restricting and i will talk about calories in versus calories out in a moment without attention to dietary quality then i think that you are moving toward an unhealthy state of weight loss an unhealthy state of being a human and you should consider that in your weight loss journey i do believe there are healthy ways for humans to lose weight these often center on focusing on dietary quality and i believe if you do that rather than focusing on restricting calories without attention to dietary quality you will lose weight unintentionally as has been demonstrated in the past with diets that include meat and fruit specifically paleo diets which have vegetables but we'll talk about those a bit later on in the podcast if you focus on dietary quality i believe you will lose weight without having to work at it too hard and that is the best way to lose weight how not to lose weight don't put yourself in a calorie restricted prison the human body our metabolism our brain the hypothalamus all of the mechanisms of the body that control satiety from the stomach to the duodenum the ghrelin system our neuroendocrine hormones our gastrointestinal hormones that feed back to the brain are exquisitely evolved over millions of years of pre-hominid evolution and hundreds of thousands of years of homo sapiens evolution to prevent us from starving if we've gotten to be obese and i will talk about why i think that happens we've gone off of our evolutionary programming in some way we've probably eaten a diet that is evolutionarily inconsistent i believe that is what drives us to get fat doing evolutionarily inconsistent things in our consumption of specific foods specific macronutrients i'll talk about those later and that leads to an evolutionarily disordered state of obesity you don't see a lot of obesity in fact you don't see any obesity when you go to hunter-gatherer tribes like the hadza who i visited last year i'll show some studies with them later in the podcast or if you go to look at the kung or other hunter gatherers there's no obesity there in fact when i went to visit the hadza there was one woman in the tribe who was overweight she was actually the the wife of the chief of the tribe and we asked the chief or we asked the tribe what happened to her in a polite way and the response we got was that she had been spending more time with missionaries in the city and eating things like cornmeal processed food processed grains and seed oils so this is a ant of one it's an interesting anecdote but it does illustrate that even hunter gatherers can get fat if they eat a westernized type diet we see this over and over when humans of all sorts of nationalities all sorts of cultures move from more evolutionarily consistent foods which can be a wide variety of things and they go on to a westernized diet pattern which is an amorphous term but i will clarify it in a moment they often become obese and they become metabolically unwell metabolically unhealthy metabolically disordered those are all synonyms insulin resistance so what is a westernized diet pattern no

chunks.title: The Role of Quality in Diet
chunks.summary: A significant portion of the podcast is dedicated to how dietary quality influences weight loss and overall health. The speaker emphasizes focusing on whole foods, particularly meat and organs, rather than processed options.

chunks.title: Impact of Nutrients on Satiety
chunks.summary: The importance of micronutrients and their role in achieving satiety is discussed. The speaker posits that insufficient micronutrient intake can lead to increased hunger and obesity.
chunks.transcript: thermodynamics yes it works if you eat more calories then you metabolize then you oxidize then you burn you will gain weight the problem with this equation is that it's very hard to active you are if you are over exercising for instance you may increase your cortisol you may lower your thyroid hormone you may increase your reverse t3 all of those things will slow your overall metabolism which will lead to a lower calories out i also think there's very interesting evidence that the quality of the foods you eat the quality of your calories in can negatively or positively affect your calories out i'll show evidence later in this podcast that seed oils polyunsaturated fatty acids of the omega-6 variety can affect thyroid hormone transport metabolism and binding at the level of the nuclear t3 receptor in a negative way so the quality of the foods you eat can affect your metabolism on the way out that's the problem with the calories in calories out model in my chunks.title: Dairy and Health Benefits
chunks.summary: The speaker addresses the role of dairy in health, referencing studies that suggest raw dairy can be associated with reduced rates of childhood allergies and obesity. The podcast discusses different types of dairy and their implications for health.

acid found in animal fats things like milk cheese butter and tallow that those mice get lean they lose weight they lose visceral adiposity and they become healthy this is one of the reasons that i'm a chunks.title: The Dangers of Seed Oils
chunks.summary: The negative health implications of consuming seed oils are explored. The speaker relates the consumption of these oils to hunger mechanisms and obesity, while advocating for a shift towards healthier fat sources.

chunks.title: Final Thoughts on Sustainable Weight Loss
chunks.summary: In conclusion, the podcast reiterates the significance of dietary quality over calorie restriction for long-term weight loss and health maintenance. It encourages listeners to prioritize whole, nutrient-dense foods to support their health goals.
